folio.label: 47r
folio.l: And he was redy , with his Iren hote
And Nicholas , in the ars he smote
Of goth the Skyn , an hanbrede a boute
The hote cultur , brent so his toute
That for the smert , he wend for to dye
As he were wode , for woo he gan to crye
Help , water water , help for goddes hert
¢ This Carpenter , ou©t of his slomer stert´
And herd on crye water , as he were wode
And seide allas , now cometú Noes Flode
He sette hi~ vp , with oute wordes moo
And with his ax , he smote the corde a two
And doun gotú al , he fond neither to selle
Brede ne Ale , tyl he cam to the Celle
Vp on the flor , and there a swou©ne he lay
Vp stirt hire Alisou~ , and Nicholay
And crieden ou©t , and herrowe , in the strete
The neyghebores , bothe smale and grete
In ronnen , for to gawren , on this man
That in swownyng lay , bothe pale and wan
For with the fal , he broken had his arme
But stonde he must , vn to his owen harme
For whan he spak , he was a non born~ dounö
With hende Nicholas , and Alisouö
They tolden euäy man , that he was wode
He was so a gast , of Noes flode
Thorugú fantasye , that of his vanyte
He had bougút hi~ , knedynge tubbes thre
And had hem hanged , in the rof a boue
And that he preyed hem , for goddes loue
To sitten in the rof , ¹ companye
The folk gonne laughen , at his fantasye
In to the rof , they kekyn and they gape
And turned al his harm , vn to a Iape
For what so æ æ this Carpenter , answered
It was for nougút , noman his resou~ hered
With othes grete , he was so sworn~ a dounö
That he was holden wod , in al the tounö
For euäy clerk , a non rigút , held witú other
And seiden , the man was wod , my lef brother
And euäy wyght gan laughen , of this striffe
Thus swyued was , the Carpenteres wyffe
For al his kepyng , and his Ielousye
And Absolouö , hath kyssed , hire nether eye
And Nicholas is skalded , in the toute
This tale is don , and god saue al the route ;

note.notetxt: quod Wytton~ ;666 In the right margin.ES

666The tale ends on the recto. Compression of the last three/four lines. What the following recto already planned and written out? L2 begins immediately at the top of 47v and no space was left for incipit and explicit. It is a caracteristic of the text of MI to be often compressed in the final lines of the folio, as if the space was limitated, as a whole, but also folio by folio.ODR
